seoTitle: Canna vs Canning Vale — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Canna and Canning Vale. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Canna vs Canning Vale: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Canna and Canning Vale.
heading: Canna vs Canning Vale — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Canna, Western Australia and Canning Vale, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Canna has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Canning Vale (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Canna or Canning Vale safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Canna has a flood risk score of 55/100, while Canning Vale scores 51/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Canna scores 47/100 for bushfire risk. Canning Vale scores 44/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Canna or Canning Vale?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Canna has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.25% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
